intro: Orbivestus karaguensis is a traditional medicinal plant, commonly known as Karagana or Desert Date, that belongs to the family Fabaceae. The plant is rich in various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Orbivestus karaguensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments to treat various health conditions, with its fruit and leaves being commonly used for medicinal purposes.
